_provenance: EDITORIAL DATA (FIREWALL.md): nothing here is a computed fact about the sport. Band colors are the published flag-specification colors — the same provenance family as the Twemoji-derived flag SVGs the packs already bundle. Accents are editorial DISPLAY colors for text/interactive use on the dark shell: where the spec color already measures WCAG AA on every shell surface it is used verbatim; where it does not (dark reds, dark cobalts), the display accent keeps the flag hue and is lightened only until it measures. Every entry is verified by validate-nations.ts — the build FAILS if any pair falls below AA.
_fallback: Any nation absent from this table — or any entry the validator rejects — renders UNTHEMED: the incumbent --team teal, no band, no banner tint. Themed rendering is an allowlist, never a derivation from image pixels.

England.note: The hard case: spec red #CE1124 measures 3.47:1 on --bg — display accent lightened along the same hue; white is reserved for the band (as text it would be indistinguishable from --text).

Brazil.note: Spec yellow passes everywhere (14.7:1 on --bg) and is the more distinctive of the two verde-amarela colors against the shell.

Argentina.note: Spec celeste passes verbatim (8.1:1 on --bg) — zero adjustment.

Germany.note: Spec gold passes verbatim (13.1:1); spec red measures 3.79:1 and black is invisible on the shell — both stay band-only. The black band stripe gets a 1px --surface inset in the mock so it reads against --bg (see DESIGN.md degrade notes).

Netherlands.note: The tricolor-gradient case. Spec cobalt #21468B measures 2.15:1 — display accent lightened along the cobalt hue. (Founder alternative: Oranje #FF8200 is the fan color but is NOT on the flag — outside the flag-spec provenance family, so not used without an explicit call.)
